core_mechanism: a linear parametric amplification (exponential two-mode squeezing instability) saturated by a nonlinearity-induced anharmonic potential along a discrete ladder of number states, producing a bounded self-limiting oscillation with a finite turning point and periodic collapse-and-revival dynamics
brief_reason: The abstract states that k-gap amplification drives two-mode squeezing of the biphoton while Kerr nonlinearity generates an anharmonic potential along the Fock ladder that balances this squeezing, creating a finite number turning point and giving rise to collapse-and-revival dynamics.
